sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Dexcom Q4 2025 beats forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: Dexcom (DXCM) reported Q4 2025 earnings exceeding forecasts with an EPS of $0.68 and revenue of $1.26 billion, yet its stock dipped 4.45% in after-hours trading. The company highlighted strong international growth, the launch of the Dexcom G7 15-day system, and anticipates Medicare coverage expansion for new patient segments. Dexcom also provided a robust 2026 revenue guidance between $5.16 billion and $5.25 billion, expecting continued growth driven by product innovation and market expansion.

feed.title: Assessing Vulcan Materials (VMC) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Strong Longer Term Gains
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC) has seen a recent share price decline despite strong longer-term gains, prompting an assessment of its valuation. While a fair value estimate suggests the stock is slightly undervalued at $319.78, its high P/E ratio of 37.6x raises questions about the margin of safety for investors. The article encourages investors to conduct their own analysis, considering both the positive narrative of infrastructure growth and potential risks like project delays or funding disruptions.

feed.title: Federal Realty (FRT) Exceeds Q4 Revenue Expectations with Robust Growth
feed.summary: Federal Realty (FRT) surpassed Q4 revenue expectations, reporting $336.04 million against an anticipated $329.02 million, driven by strong leasing and rent increases. The company is actively pursuing strategic objectives including capital recycling, reinvestment, and expansion into new markets, despite refinancing challenges. Federal Realty forecasts a 6% increase in Core FFO by 2026, supported by its strategic initiatives and market position in high-growth metropolitan areas.

feed.title: Cardinal Health Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.5107 per Share, Payable April 15 to Holders of Record April 1
feed.summary: Cardinal Health announced its quarterly dividend will remain at $0.5107 per share. The dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026. This news comes amidst various analyst target price adjustments and a maintained 'Buy' or 'Outperform' rating from several firms.

feed.title: Applied Materials jumps on earnings strength and bullish forecast tied to AI chip boom
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. stock soared over 12% in after-hours trading following a strong fiscal first-quarter report that surpassed analyst expectations for both earnings and revenue. The semiconductor equipment maker's positive outlook for the coming quarter, driven by the increasing demand for AI chips, also contributed to the bullish sentiment. CEO Gary Dickerson anticipates over 20% growth in their semiconductor equipment business this calendar year, focusing on high-performance and energy-efficient chips.

feed.title: Prologis Board of Directors Approves 6 Percent Increase in Quarterly Common Stock Dividend
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc.'s Board of Directors has approved a 6% increase in the company's annualized dividend, raising it to $4.28 per share of common stock. The board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.07 per common share and $1.0675 per share of 8.54% Series Q Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock, both payable on March 31, 2026. These dividends are for stockholders of record as of March 17, 2026.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Files for Mixed Securities Shelf Off
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) has filed an automatic mixed securities shelf registration, giving it flexibility to raise capital. While the company shows strong revenue growth and a good market position in the Aerospace & Defense sector, investors should note its declining margins and high valuation metrics. This strategic financial move allows L3Harris to offer various types of securities for future initiatives, amidst concerns about its profitability and market sentiment.

feed.title: Hormel Foods (HRL) Appoints New Head of Supply Chain Operations
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has appointed Will Bonifant as Group Vice President and Chief Supply Chain Officer, effective March 9. Bonifant, previously from Hershey, will oversee Hormel's global supply chain operations, including procurement, manufacturing, logistics, and innovation. This strategic hire aims to enhance the company's supply chain efficiency and innovation as Hormel continues to evolve its product offerings in the consumer packaged goods sector.

feed.title: Jeanine Pirro sues Con-Ed , NY town over trip at construction site that caused her brain bruising
feed.summary: Jeanine Pirro is suing Con Edison and the town of Rye, New York, claiming a "dangerous" construction site caused her to trip and suffer a brain contusion and other injuries last August. The lawsuit alleges negligence on the part of the company and the town, where Pirro resides. She is seeking damages for pain, suffering, inconvenience, and other losses, but the specific amount is not disclosed.

feed.title: Jeanine Pirro files $250K injury lawsuit against City of Rye, Con Edison
feed.summary: Jeanine Pirro has filed a $250,000 injury lawsuit against the City of Rye and Con Edison. She claims that a preventable injury left her bedridden. The lawsuit seeks damages for the injuries sustained.

feed.title: NASCAR® and Coca-Cola North America Extend Longstanding Partnership; BODYARMOR Named Official Sports Drink of NASCAR
feed.summary: NASCAR and Coca-Cola North America have extended their long-standing Premier Partnership, with BODYARMOR Sports Drink now named the Official Sports Drink of NASCAR. This multi-year agreement expands Coca-Cola's involvement in the sport, integrating BODYARMOR into NASCAR's hydration category while maintaining Coca-Cola's designation as the Official Soft Drink. The partnership will feature robust activation platforms, including at-track experiences and expanded storytelling, and continue community impact initiatives.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial (NYSE:AMP) CEO William Truscott Sells 5,000 Shares
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial CEO William Truscott sold 5,000 shares of company stock for over $2.66 million, reducing his stake by 29.16%. The sale followed strong quarterly results where the company beat analyst expectations for EPS and revenue. Analysts currently have a mixed view on AMP, with a consensus target price of $555.67.

feed.title: PTC (NASDAQ:PTC) EVP Sells $38,664.00 in Stock
feed.summary: PTC's EVP Staats Aaron Von sold 240 shares of PTC stock for $38,664.00 on February 11th, reducing his ownership by 1.07% to 22,221 shares. The company recently exceeded quarterly earnings estimates with an EPS of $1.92 and revenue of $685.8 million, also providing optimistic Q2 2026 guidance. Despite a recent stock performance dip, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $188.08 for PTC.

feed.title: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) Posts Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $1.63 EPS
feed.summary: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) reported earnings of $2.60 per share for the quarter, missing the consensus estimate of $4.23 by $1.63. Despite missing EPS estimates, the company's revenue of $1.22 billion slightly exceeded analyst expectations of $1.21 billion. The stock traded up 0.3% to $294.67 on Thursday with higher-than-average volume, and Wall Street analysts currently have a "Hold" rating with a consensus target price of $306.63.

feed.title: Benjamin Jackson Sells 12,861 Shares of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) Stock
feed.summary: Benjamin Jackson, President of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE), sold 12,861 shares of company stock for over $2.12 million on February 10th, reducing his ownership by 7.20%. Despite this insider selling, ICE recently exceeded earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $1.71 versus an estimated $1.67 and revenue of $3.14 billion. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share and maintains a consensus "Buy" rating from Wall Street analysts with an average target price of $195.40.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Emerson Electric (NYSE:EMR) Director Sells 1,476 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Emerson Electric's Director Matthew Levatich sold 1,476 shares of EMR stock for $239,348 on February 10th, decreasing his stake by 5.72%. This comes as EMR shares dropped 6.2% intraday and the company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.555, alongside beating EPS estimates and issuing FY2026 guidance. Institutional ownership remains high, with analysts holding a consensus "Hold" rating for the stock.

feed.title: Mark Begor Sells 37,791 Shares of Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Stock
feed.summary: Equifax CEO Mark Begor sold 37,791 shares of the company's stock for approximately $7.63 million, reducing his holding by 14.09%. This insider sale comes as Equifax shares dropped by 4.7% to $188.30, trading below their 50-day and 200-day moving averages, despite the company beating Q4 earnings estimates and setting optimistic FY2026 guidance. Analysts currently maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $240.33, though some have recently lowered their price objectives.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) EVP Sells 1,167 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Executive Vice President Jamil Farshchi sold 1,167 shares of the company's stock on February 11th, totaling approximately $233,248. This sale reduced his holdings by 2.84% to 39,869 shares. The transaction follows a previous sale of 6,213 shares on February 10th and comes as Equifax exceeded Q4 earnings estimates, set favorable FY2026 EPS guidance, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells 5,662 Shares
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Patrick Plewman sold 5,662 shares of the company's stock for over $1.15 million on February 10th, reducing his ownership by 26.91%. This comes after Quest Diagnostics reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, raised its dividend, and authorized a $1 billion share buyback program. Analysts have largely maintained "Buy" or "Hold" ratings with an average target price of $208.93.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group prices $1.25B senior notes due 2033 and $1.25B senior notes due 2038
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has priced two offerings of senior notes: $1.25 billion due in 2033 and another $1.25 billion due in 2038. This financial move aims to raise capital for the cruise line operator.

feed.title: Blackstone Leads the Race to Unlock $7 Trillion of Cash in Japan
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. is leading a campaign to attract Japanese private wealth by promoting alternative asset investments. With a significant media presence, including a television commercial featuring co-founder Steve Schwarzman, Blackstone aims to tap into the $7 trillion in cash held by Japanese households. This effort, alongside rivals like EQT AB and KKR & Co., targets the largest private wealth opportunity outside the US.

feed.title: Snap-on Incorporated Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Snap-on Incorporated announced that its board of directors declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $2.44 per share. This dividend is payable on March 10, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 24, 2026. The company has a long history of paying consecutive quarterly cash dividends since 1939.

feed.title: Alexandria Equities Prices $750 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE) has successfully priced a $750 million offering of 5.25% senior notes due in 2036. This underwritten offering, which is fully guaranteed, is expected to settle around February 25, 2026, and will be used primarily to repay commercial paper borrowings associated with a previous cash tender offer for older senior notes. This strategic move aims to extend debt maturities and enhance the company's balance-sheet flexibility by reducing reliance on short-term funding.

feed.title: Live Nation Break Up Is Less Likely After DOJ Overhaul. What Would a Deal Look Like?
feed.summary: Following the departure of Assistant Attorney General Gail Slater, Live Nation might be closer to an out-of-court settlement with the Department of Justice regarding its monopoly suit. The potential deal is unlikely to involve a full breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster, but rather smaller concessions such as divestitures in artist management, an end to exclusivity agreements, or enhanced pricing transparency. This development comes despite calls from groups like NIVA for a complete dissolution of the Live Nation-Ticketmaster merger, with many states that joined the lawsuit still expected to pursue litigation if a settlement is reached.

feed.title: Wells Fargo CFO expects more layoffs after cutting tens of thousands of workers
feed.summary: Wells Fargo CFO Mike Santomassimo announced that the bank anticipates further layoffs and real estate sales. Since CEO Charlie Scharf took over in October 2019, Wells Fargo has already laid off approximately 70,000 employees, consistently reducing staff every quarter for over five years. This continued shedding of staff indicates an ongoing restructuring or cost-cutting effort within the company.

feed.title: Republic Services Appoints New Director and Declares Dividend
feed.summary: Republic Services (RSG) announced the appointment of Ian Craig, CEO of Coca-Cola FEMSA, to its board of directors, increasing the board to 13 members and adding expertise in international, digital, and sustainability areas. Concurrently, the board declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.625 per share, payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 2, 2026. An AI analyst rates RSG as "Outperform," highlighting its financial performance and sustainability investments while noting potential overvaluation and challenges in recycling prices.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Aethlon Medical Q3 2026 sees operational strides amid financial challenges
feed.summary: Aethlon Medical (AEMD) reported its Q3 2026 financial results, showcasing operational progress in its Australian oncology trials despite an increase in operating expenses. The company's stock experienced a 6.7% decline, reflecting investor concerns over financial metrics and future projections. Aethlon is advancing its Hemopurifier platform, exploring new applications, and managing costs while targeting a Data Safety Monitoring Board review by late March.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Appoints John W. Chidsey President and CEO
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) has announced the appointment of John W. Chidsey as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately. Chidsey, previously a board member and former CEO of Subway Restaurants and Burger King, succeeds Harry Sommer. The board expressed confidence that Chidsey's leadership will drive operational rigor, improve financial performance, and enhance long-term shareholder value.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings CEO Harry Sommer Departs; Former Subway CEO John Chidsey Takes Charge
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. has announced a leadership change, with former Subway CEO John W. Chidsey taking over as president and CEO, effective immediately. He replaces Harry Sommer, who is stepping down from his role and the Board of Directors. Chidsey, previously a board member, is expected to drive operational efficiency and growth, leaning on his experience in transforming major consumer-facing companies.

feed.title: XP Inc. Reshapes Control Structure as New Executives Join XP Control LLC
feed.summary: XP Inc. is reorganizing the ownership of its controlling entity, XP Control LLC, by adding senior executives Thiago Maffra and José Berenguer as voting partners and buying out three original partners' stakes. Despite a reduction in direct economic interest to 18% of Class A shares, XP Control LLC will maintain at least 69% of XP's voting power, with founder Guilherme Benchimol remaining the majority unitholder. This strategic realignment aims to enhance governance, stabilize the control structure, and support long-term value creation for stakeholders.

feed.title: Dover Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Dover Corporation (NYSE: DOV) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.52 per share. This dividend is payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026. Dover is a diversified global manufacturer based in Downers Grove, Illinois, with annual revenue exceeding $8 billion.

feed.title: Citigroup Board Sets $42 Million Pay for CEO
feed.summary: Citigroup's board has approved a $42 million compensation package for CEO Jane Fraser for 2025. This decision reflects the company's record 2025 revenues, improved returns, and leading stock performance, as well as progress in regulatory remediation and business simplification. The pay structure emphasizes deferred equity and performance units, signaling the board's confidence in Citi's ongoing transformation and focus on shareholder value.

feed.title: Xilio Therapeutics Prices Pre-Funded Warrant Offering Financing
feed.summary: Xilio Therapeutics announced the pricing of an underwritten offering of pre-funded warrants, expected to generate approximately $37.1 million in net proceeds. The financing, backed by Coastlands Capital, Gilead Sciences, OrbiMed, and Perceptive Advisors, aims to fund its cancer drug pipeline and general corporate purposes, extending its cash runway into 2027. Despite strong revenue growth, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates XLO as Neutral due to weak financial fundamentals.

feed.title: Home prices inch higher, buyers and sellers find balance, Homes.com finds
feed.summary: A new report from Homes.com, a CoStar Group company, indicates that U.S. home prices saw a 1.3% year-over-year increase in January 2026, with the median sale price reaching $374,900. The report highlights an improved balance between buyers and sellers, characterized by normal inventory levels and median days on the market. Growth was strongest in Northeast and Midwestern markets, while some Southern and Western cities experienced price declines.

feed.title: Google confirms $10B Project Mica in Northland
feed.summary: Google has confirmed its plan to build the enormous $10 billion Project Mica data center campus in Kansas City's Northland area, solidifying one of the city's largest development deals. Under an agreement with Evergy, Google will cover the full energy costs for the facility, which could require up to 500 megawatts of power. The project includes significant upfront workforce funding for local school districts and is expected to support numerous construction jobs, though details on permanent positions and water usage remain to be fully disclosed.

feed.title: Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) Sets New 52-Week Low - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) shares hit a new 52-week low of $1,281.01 during trading. Despite the dip, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $2,071, indicating significant upside potential. The company's fundamentals showed a mixed picture, with a beat on quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, but recent insider stock sales.

feed.title: Zenas BioPharma (NASDAQ:ZBIO) Trading 13.8% Higher - What's Next?
feed.summary: Zenas BioPharma (NASDAQ:ZBIO) saw its stock price rise by 13.8% during trading, closing at $26.48 with reduced trading volume. Analysts have issued new price targets for ZBIO, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $43.14. Insider transactions show CEO Leon O. Moulder, Jr. recently increased his stake in the company.

feed.title: Federal Realty Trust Q4 earnings just miss, as comp POI growth slows (FRT:NYSE)
feed.summary: Federal Realty Trust (FRT) reported Q4 earnings that slightly missed Wall Street expectations in terms of core FFO per share, but revenue exceeded forecasts. The retail REIT saw a 1.7% dip in its stock during after-hours trading. Despite the slight miss, comparable portfolio occupancy and leased rates improved, and the company projects approximately 6% core FFO growth for the upcoming year.

feed.title: T-Mobile Agrees to Sell €2.5 Billion of Euro-Denominated Senior Notes
feed.summary: T-Mobile USA, Inc. announced its agreement to sell €2.5 billion in euro-denominated senior notes across three tranches maturing in 2032, 2035, and 2038. The offering is expected to close on February 19, 2026, with net proceeds intended for general corporate purposes, including share repurchases, dividends, and refinancing existing debt. Numerous financial institutions are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: Columbus Circle Capital raises $230 million in IPO By Investing.com
feed.summary: Columbus Circle Capital Corp. II successfully completed its initial public offering, raising $230 million by selling 23 million units at $10 each, including units from the over-allotment option. The units, which began trading on Nasdaq on February 11, 2026, consist of Class A ordinary shares and redeemable warrants. Cohen & Company Capital Markets led the offering for the blank check company, which plans to pursue mergers or acquisitions.

feed.title: Ashford Hospitality Sells Houston Hotel, Reduces Debt Load
feed.summary: Ashford Hospitality Trust (AHT) recently sold its 150-room Embassy Suites Houston Near the Galleria for approximately $13.1 million in net cash proceeds. The majority of these proceeds, about $12.9 million, were used to reduce a mortgage loan covering 14 other hotels, contributing to a leaner balance sheet and a non-recurring gain. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates AHT as Neutral due to weak financial performance despite improving free cash flow and positive short-term technicals.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries shares jump as Q4 results top estimates By Investing.com
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries (NYSE:MHK) reported Q4 earnings and revenue that surpassed analyst expectations, causing its shares to rise 4% in after-hours trading. The company posted adjusted EPS of $2.00 on revenue of $2.7 billion, driven by productivity and restructuring efforts, despite market pricing pressures. Mohawk anticipates improved sales and earnings in Q1 2026 due to strengthening economies and lower mortgage rates.

feed.title: Columbus Circle Capital raises $230 million in IPO on NASDAQ
feed.summary: Columbus Circle Capital Corp. II (CMIIU) successfully completed its initial public offering, raising $230 million by selling 23 million units at $10 each, including an over-allotment option exercise. The company's units began trading on NASDAQ Global Market on February 11, 2026, with Class A ordinary shares and warrants expected to list separately later. This blank check company aims to pursue mergers or acquisitions, led by CEO Gary Quin and CFO Joseph W. Pooler, Jr.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries shares jump as Q4 results top estimates By Investing.com
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries (NYSE:MHK) reported Q4 earnings and revenue that surpassed analyst expectations, leading to a 4% rise in after-hours trading. The flooring manufacturer posted adjusted EPS of $2.00 against an estimated $1.98, and revenue of $2.7 billion, exceeding the $2.68 billion consensus. The company anticipates improved sales and earnings in Q1 2026, driven by strengthening economies and lower mortgage rates.

feed.title: How FedEx plans to continue transformation, hit financial goals
feed.summary: During its Investor Day in Memphis, FedEx outlined its strategy for continued transformation and financial growth. The company plans to prioritize premium growth in high-margin sectors, enhance its digital and AI capabilities, and reshape its network to boost profits and stockholder value.

feed.title: Trump's FTC chairman chides Apple boss Tim Cook for content of Apple news feed
feed.summary: The chairman of the Federal Trade Commission, Andrew Ferguson, has written to Apple CEO Tim Cook to express concerns that Apple News is suppressing content from conservative news outlets. Ferguson's complaint follows a report by the Media Research Center alleging that the news feed disproportionately features "leftist" sources while omitting prominent conservative media. This criticism comes despite Cook's attendance at President Trump's second term inauguration.

feed.title: Public Storage forecast full-year core FFO below estimates, CEO Joe Russell to depart
feed.summary: Public Storage announced a full-year core FFO forecast that is below analyst estimates. Additionally, CEO Joe Russell is set to depart from the company. This news was reported by Refinitiv.

feed.title: Country star Ella Langley named American Eagle’s new denim spokesperson
feed.summary: Country music star Ella Langley has been named the new spokesperson for American Eagle's "Denim Darling" ad campaign. This role comes after her significant success in country music, including winning multiple awards at the American Country Music Awards. The campaign launches shortly after American Eagle faced controversy over its previous "Sydney Sweeney Has Great Jeans" campaign, which drew criticism for alleged racial undertones.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Issues $3.8 Billion Notes for Acquisition
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) has issued $3.8 billion in senior unsecured notes across four tranches to fund a portion of its acquisition of Clario Holdings, Inc. The company expects net proceeds of approximately $3.76 billion. While the immediate use is for the acquisition, funds may temporarily be allocated to general corporate purposes or short-term investments, demonstrating Thermo Fisher's strategy of using debt markets for strategic M&A and financial flexibility.

feed.title: $40M Tucows buyback to retire shares over the next year
feed.summary: Tucows Inc. (NASDAQ: TCX) has announced a new $40 million open-market stock buyback program, set to commence on February 13, 2026, and conclude by February 12, 2027. This program will involve repurchasing common shares via the Nasdaq Capital Market, with all acquired shares being retired and returned to treasury. The initiative follows the termination of a previous $40 million buyback program and will be funded through available working capital and existing credit facilities, with repurchases dependent on cash and market conditions.
